response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship, open-source leadership, and some high-throughput distributed systems exposure, but he falls well short of the role's core minimum qualifications. His C++ experience is limited to roughly one year (National Instruments and a thesis project), far below the required 8 years, and his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than cloud compute platforms. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at scale.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Broad full-stack and infrastructure skills including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, and AWS CDK
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing via workshops, courses, and long-running open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 1 year versus the required 8 years
No formal technical-lead role or experience leading cloud platform strategy across teams
Primary depth is in web/product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not cloud compute infrastructure or hardware architecture
